title: The case for MUDs in modern times
excerpt: In the age of smart devices and instant entertainment, can a medium as old as MUDs survive? Driven entirely by text, these Multi-User Dimensions ― or Dungeon...
content_html:
In the age of smart devices and instant entertainment, can a medium as old as MUDs survive? Driven entirely by text, these Multi-User Dimensions ― or Dungeons, or Domains, or so many other options that they’re often called MU* ― are virtual worlds that consist entirely of text. Often outfitted as RPG-style games, these platforms are also home to niche internet communities on the fringes of the web. The servers are accessed via a telnet client, which is easy enough to launch in a command line. But there are a few popular applications that many longtime players opt to use. They’re rather reminiscent of the various chat programs built up around the IRC protocol, and provide simple functionality like logging, colors, aliases, triggers, timers, and even mapping.

At this point, if you’ve never played a MUD before, you might be very confused by this prospect. An online game that’s entirely text? How does it work? More importantly, why would anyone play that?

When I first stumbled across MUDs as a preteen, that was my initial reaction. I was an avid reader, but the idea of playing a game that was entirely text felt more like work than fun. Perhaps it did feel like work a bit in the beginning, but I picked up the controls pretty quickly, and soon things felt very natural to navigate. In a typical MUD setting, you are represented as a textual avatar inside of a space called a room. A room has a written description and a list of objects that are inside that room. A room can be anything: a lion’s den, outer space, an old crypt, a classroom, a wide prarie, or even a coral reef! Your avatar can interact with the game world and its contents via commands you enter. These can be as simple as 'get sword' but can be as expansive as any imperative clause. Some MUDs have robust command systems that allow you to be verbose, like 'look at gemstones on tiara' or 'pilot ship 500 meters north', and intricate complexity can follow.

These concepts will be very familiar to you if you’ve ever played interactive fiction (IF), which can be best described as the single-player version of MUDs. There are many similarities between IF and MUDs, but the latter is focused on collaborative (or competitive) effort in a realtime environment.

In a MUD, the environment is constantly changing, producing a sense of place by reacting to stimuli. As a virtual world, it simulates various systems with varying levels of complexity, depending on the codebase and the design of the environment. This ever-churning simulation is the heart of what makes a MUD so uniquely interesting. These systems can simulate time, weather, economies, NPC schedules, and more ― all of which work together to create an interconnected environment that can be navigated by your avatar and interacted with via verbs. The intricacies of these simulations can vary wildly between games and different codebases. Some MUDs provide a more straightforward hack-n-slash environment where players collect items and kill monsters to gain experience and get stronger. These types of games are essentially programmed rulebooks, replacing the role of a GM (Game Master) in a tabletop game and instead handling all of the combat, dice rolling, skill building, and inventory management that you would otherwise be doing with pen and paper around a table. Other MUDs provide a completely freeform environment, with no rules, items, or NPCs. Players in these environments are provided a canvas and often expected to roleplay their interactions within the environment, collaboratively writing a story with other players ― with or without the interference of game administrators. Some MUDs fall somewhere in the middle, leveraging the best of both worlds to provide unique experiences.

As computers grow more interconnected with everyday facets of our lives, they further become tools through which we can manipulate the real world around us. A surgeon can perform surgery from the other side of the planet with robotic arms and a camera, for example. The unique sensation and phenomenon of interacting with the world through technology can bestow a sence presence that’s come to be defined as telepresence. Telepresence refers to the experience of controlling objects remotely, and the sensation people have when they interact with and immerse themselves within a virtual environment. In the study of telepresence, scholars such as Rheingold have cited MUDs as being “imaginary worlds in computer databases where people use words and programming languages to improvise melodramas, build worlds and all the objects in them, solve puzzles, invent amusements and tools, compete for prestige and power, gain wisdom, seek revenge, indulge greed and lust and violent impulses.” Turkle similarly wrote that “MUDs are a new kind of virtual parlor game and a new form of community. In addition, text-based MUDs are a new form of collaboratively written literature. MUD players are MUD authors, the creators as well as consumers of media content. In this, participating in a MUD has much in common with script writing, performance art, street theater, improvisational theater ― or even commedia dell’arte.”

This unique sensation of telepresence can come in six unique forms, depending on the user’s level of immersion and their expectations of the virtual environment in which they’re navigating. These telepresence senses include social richness (the feeling one gets from social interaction), realism (such as computer-generated environments looking, feeling, or otherwise seeming real), transportation (also includes users feeling as though something is “here” with them or feeling as though they are sharing common space with another person together), immersion (either through the senses or through the mind), being a social actor within the medium (no longer passive viewers, gaining a sense of interactivity and control), and the medium itself as a social actor.

MUDs are also home to many players with vision impairments, who are attracted to the environment because they can potentially experience it fully. Visually-impaired players are often unable to play games that rely heavily on graphics, because they can struggle to interpret what is happening on the screen. However, in a text-based games, visually-impaired players can use a screenreader to read the contents of the terminal as events happen live on the screen. Many MUDs provide configuration options for visually-impaired players, allowing them to disable nonsensical text like ASCII art or character-based maps, and streamlining the text so that a screenreader can effectively convey the information to the player. A visually-impaired player with a high recitation speed on their screenreader can play just as effectively as a someone reading their screen, using sounds and triggers to facilitate their gameplay.

In fact, until the invention of graphics cards and technologies that surpass the human imagination (haha!), MUDs will be difficult to completely remove from the scene. Imagine a realtime adventure with other people online, as vividly detailed as your favorite book but with all of the agency in your hands? That’s the reality of gameplay on a MUD. We put a lot of emphasis on graphics and visuals for games now, but as hardware begins to plateau, avenues like MUDs will continue to double-down on the experiences that make them so great, eschewing the bridles of resource creation in exchange for pure poiesis. You can spin up a MUD and start writing the environment, making it as intricate as you’d like, and everything you describe costs you the same amount of effort: words. Making a game with graphics is entirely different, with various effects, models, and landscapes requiring more time, attention, and skills than others. In a graphic-based game, not every asset has the same cost associated, and this can place artificial limitations on a game. Developers may opt for a less dramatic or exciting route simply because they lack the time or budget. Whereas in developing a MUD, your only limitation is your ability to describe.

Despite the age and legacy of this type of program, starting a MUD in 2018 can be a difficult process. There are many popular codebases that have been in use for years or even decades, and the command syntax, interfaces, and unique quirks of each of them means that players often develop preferences. These codebases can sometimes be in older programming languages or require complex accompanying databases to facilitate the program. Thankfully, there are codebases available that are fairly straightforward to set up and come with a fair amount of functionality out of the box. It’s worth noting that it can easily take months or years of development for even a team of dedicated creators to build a quality, developed MUD for players, and that’s barely considering the amount of writing and building that has to go into it. Creating unique and compelling gameplay mechanics that are balanced and engaging is a mixture of art and science that many MUDs still struggle to get right. Because I am a JavaScript junkie, I’ve done a fair amount of tinkering in a MUD engine called Ranvier and I recommend this codebase for modern would-be MUD wizards looking to set up a _tabula rasa _for tinkering. Because it takes such a large amount of time and dedication to create a MUD, I do not think there are any released/popular MUDs out there currently using the Ranvier engine, but the same can be said for many MUD codebases, unfortunately. Due to the nature of niche communities in which MUDs tend to live, it’s fairly common for these virtual worlds to appear, amass players, and then vanish just as quickly as they came. In some cases, the community can recover the source code and try to reboot, but other times the entire content is lost forever. Still, you can find a wide variety of popular MUDS ― some of which have been around for decades ― on websites like Top Mud Sites and The Mud Connector and I encourage you to check one out if any of this piques your interest.

You can read more about my new MUD engine project, Pinwheel, here on my blog.

Further Reading

MUD - Wikipedia

MUSH - Wikipedia

MOO - Wikipedia

Telnet - Wikipedia

[Telepresence - Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Presence_(telepresence)

Ranvier, a MUD engine in JavaScript

blocks.html_en: In the age of smart devices and instant entertainment, can a medium as old as MUDs survive? Driven entirely by text, these Multi-User Dimensions ― or Dungeons, or Domains, or so many other options that they’re often called MU* ― are virtual worlds that consist entirely of text. sentences.en: In the age of smart devices and instant entertainment, can a medium as old as MUDs survive?
sentences.zh: 在智能设备和即时娱乐的时代，MUD这样古老的媒介能否生存？

sentences.en: Driven entirely by text, these Multi-User Dimensions ― or Dungeons, or Domains, or so many other options that they’re often called MU* ― are virtual worlds that consist entirely of text.
sentences.zh: 这些多用户维度（或地下城、域或许多其他选项，通常被称为 MU*）完全由文本驱动，是完全由文本组成的虚拟世界。

sentences.en: Often outfitted as RPG-style games, these platforms are also home to niche internet communities on the fringes of the web.
sentences.zh: 这些平台通常被设计成角色扮演游戏，同时也是网络边缘小众互联网社区的所在地。

sentences.en: The servers are accessed via a telnet client, which is easy enough to launch in a command line.
sentences.zh: 这些服务器是通过 telnet 客户端访问的，该客户端很容易在命令行中启动。

sentences.en: But there are a few popular applications that many longtime players opt to use.
sentences.zh: 但有一些流行的应用程序是许多长期玩家选择使用的。

sentences.en: They’re rather reminiscent of the various chat programs built up around the IRC protocol, and provide simple functionality like logging, colors, aliases, triggers, timers, and even mapping.
sentences.zh: 它们很容易让人想起围绕 IRC 协议构建的各种聊天程序，并提供简单的功能，如日志记录、颜色、别名、触发器、计时器，甚至映射。

blocks.html_en: At this point, if you’ve never played a MUD before, you might be very confused by this prospect. An online game that’s entirely text? How does it work? More importantly, why would anyone play that?

sentences.en: At this point, if you’ve never played a MUD before, you might be very confused by this prospect.
sentences.zh: 此时，如果你以前从未玩过MUD，你可能会对这个前景感到非常困惑。

sentences.en: An online game that’s entirely text?
sentences.zh: 完全是文字的在线游戏？

sentences.en: How does it work?
sentences.zh: 它是如何运作的？

sentences.en: More importantly, why would anyone play that?
sentences.zh: 更重要的是，为什么有人会玩这个？

sentences.en: When I first stumbled across MUDs as a preteen, that was my initial reaction.
sentences.zh: 当我十几岁的时候第一次偶然发现 MUD 时，这是我的第一反应。

sentences.en: I was an avid reader, but the idea of playing a game that was entirely text felt more like work than fun.
sentences.zh: 我是一个狂热的读者，但玩一款完全由文字组成的游戏的想法感觉更像是工作而不是乐趣。

sentences.en: Perhaps it did feel like work a bit in the beginning, but I picked up the controls pretty quickly, and soon things felt very natural to navigate.
sentences.zh: 也许一开始确实感觉有点像工作，但我很快就掌握了控件，很快就感觉导航非常自然。

sentences.en: In a typical MUD setting, you are represented as a textual avatar inside of a space called a room.
sentences.zh: 在典型的 MUD 设置中，您被表示为一个称为房间的空间内的文本化身。

sentences.en: A room has a written description and a list of objects that are inside that room.
sentences.zh: 房间有书面描述和房间内的物体列表。

sentences.en: A room can be anything: a lion’s den, outer space, an old crypt, a classroom, a wide prarie, or even a coral reef!
sentences.zh: 房间可以是任何东西：狮子的巢穴、外太空、古老的地下室、教室、广阔的草原，甚至是珊瑚礁！

sentences.en: Your avatar can interact with the game world and its contents via commands you enter.
sentences.zh: 您的化身可以通过您输入的命令与游戏世界及其内容进行交互。

sentences.en: These can be as simple as 'get sword' but can be as expansive as any imperative clause.
sentences.zh: 这些可以像“拿剑”一样简单，但也可以像任何祈使句一样广泛。

sentences.en: Some MUDs have robust command systems that allow you to be verbose, like 'look at gemstones on tiara' or 'pilot ship 500 meters north', and intricate complexity can follow.
sentences.zh: 一些 MUD 拥有强大的命令系统，允许您进行冗长的命令，例如“查看头饰上的宝石”或“驾驶船向北 500 米”，然后复杂的情况就会随之而来。

sentences.en: These concepts will be very familiar to you if you’ve ever played interactive fiction (IF), which can be best described as the single-player version of MUDs.
sentences.zh: 如果您曾经玩过互动小说 (IF)，那么您会对这些概念非常熟悉，互动小说可以最好地描述为 MUD 的单人游戏版本。

sentences.en: There are many similarities between IF and MUDs, but the latter is focused on collaborative (or competitive) effort in a realtime environment.
sentences.zh: IF 和 MUD 之间有许多相似之处，但后者专注于实时环境中的协作（或竞争）工作。

sentences.zh: 在 MUD 中，环境不断变化，通过对刺激做出反应来产生场所感。

sentences.en: Players in these environments are provided a canvas and often expected to roleplay their interactions within the environment, collaboratively writing a story with other players ― with or without the interference of game administrators.
sentences.zh: 这些环境中的玩家被提供了一个画布，并且通常期望在环境中进行角色扮演，与其他玩家协作编写故事——无论是否有游戏管理员的干预。

sentences.en: Some MUDs fall somewhere in the middle, leveraging the best of both worlds to provide unique experiences.
sentences.zh: 有些 MUD 介于两者之间，利用两全其美来提供独特的体验。

blocks.html_en: As computers grow more interconnected with everyday facets of our lives, they further become tools through which we can manipulate the real world around us. A surgeon can perform surgery from the other side of the planet with robotic arms and a camera, for example. The unique sensation and phenomenon of interacting with the world through technology can bestow a sence presence that’s come to be defined as telepresence. Telepresence refers to the experience of controlling objects remotely, and the sensation people have when they interact with and immerse themselves within a virtual environment. In the study of telepresence, scholars such as Rheingold have cited MUDs as being “imaginary worlds in computer databases where people use words and programming languages to improvise melodramas, build worlds and all the objects in them, solve puzzles, invent amusements and tools, compete for prestige and power, gain wisdom, seek revenge, indulge greed and lust and violent impulses.” Turkle similarly wrote that “MUDs are a new kind of virtual parlor game and a new form of community. In addition, text-based MUDs are a new form of collaboratively written literature. MUD players are MUD authors, the creators as well as consumers of media content. In this, participating in a MUD has much in common with script writing, performance art, street theater, improvisational theater ― or even commedia dell’arte.”

sentences.en: As computers grow more interconnected with everyday facets of our lives, they further become tools through which we can manipulate the real world around us.
sentences.zh: 随着计算机与我们日常生活的联系越来越紧密，它们进一步成为我们操纵周围现实世界的工具。

sentences.en: A surgeon can perform surgery from the other side of the planet with robotic arms and a camera, for example.
sentences.zh: 例如，外科医生可以使用机械臂和相机从地球的另一侧进行手术。

sentences.en: The unique sensation and phenomenon of interacting with the world through technology can bestow a sence presence that’s come to be defined as telepresence.
sentences.zh: 通过技术与世界互动的独特感觉和现象可以赋予一种被定义为远程呈现的感觉存在。

sentences.en: Telepresence refers to the experience of controlling objects remotely, and the sensation people have when they interact with and immerse themselves within a virtual environment.
sentences.zh: 远程呈现是指远程控制物体的体验，以及人们与虚拟环境互动并沉浸在虚拟环境中时的感觉。

sentences.en: In the study of telepresence, scholars such as Rheingold have cited MUDs as being “imaginary worlds in computer databases where people use words and programming languages to improvise melodramas, build worlds and all the objects in them, solve puzzles, invent amusements and tools, compete for prestige and power, gain wisdom, seek revenge, indulge greed and lust and violent impulses.”
sentences.zh: 在远程呈现的研究中，莱茵戈尔德等学者将MUD称为“计算机数据库中的想象世界，人们在其中使用文字和编程语言即兴创作情节剧，构建世界及其中的所有物体，解决谜题，发明娱乐和工具，争夺声望和权力，获得智慧，寻求复仇，放纵贪婪、欲望和暴力冲动。”

sentences.en: Turkle similarly wrote that “MUDs are a new kind of virtual parlor game and a new form of community.
sentences.zh: Turkle 同样写道，“MUD 是一种新型的虚拟客厅游戏，也是一种新的社区形式。

sentences.en: In addition, text-based MUDs are a new form of collaboratively written literature.
sentences.zh: 此外，基于文本的 MUD 是一种新的协作撰写文学形式。

sentences.en: MUD players are MUD authors, the creators as well as consumers of media content.
sentences.zh: MUD玩家是MUD的作者、创作者以及媒体内容的消费者。

sentences.en: In this, participating in a MUD has much in common with script writing, performance art, street theater, improvisational theater ― or even commedia dell’arte.”
sentences.zh: 在这一点上，参与 MUD 与剧本创作、表演艺术、街头戏剧、即兴戏剧，甚至即兴喜剧有很多共同点。”

blocks.html_en: This unique sensation of telepresence can come in six unique forms, depending on the user’s level of immersion and their expectations of the virtual environment in which they’re navigating. These telepresence senses include social richness (the feeling one gets from social interaction), realism (such as computer-generated environments looking, feeling, or otherwise seeming real), transportation (also includes users feeling as though something is “here” with them or feeling as though they are sharing common space with another person together), immersion (either through the senses or through the mind), being a social actor within the medium (no longer passive viewers, gaining a sense of interactivity and control), and the medium itself as a social actor.

sentences.en: This unique sensation of telepresence can come in six unique forms, depending on the user’s level of immersion and their expectations of the virtual environment in which they’re navigating.
sentences.zh: 这种独特的远程呈现感觉可以有六种独特的形式，具体取决于用户的沉浸程度以及他们对所在虚拟环境的期望。

sentences.en: These telepresence senses include social richness (the feeling one gets from social interaction), realism (such as computer-generated environments looking, feeling, or otherwise seeming real), transportation (also includes users feeling as though something is “here” with them or feeling as though they are sharing common space with another person together), immersion (either through the senses or through the mind), being a social actor within the medium (no longer passive viewers, gaining a sense of interactivity and control), and the medium itself as a social actor.
sentences.zh: 这些远程临场感包括社交丰富性（人们从社交互动中获得的感觉）、真实感（例如计算机生成的环境看起来、感觉或以其他方式看起来真实）、交通（还包括用户感觉好像有东西在他们身边或感觉好像他们正在与另一个人共享公共空间）、沉浸感（通过感官或通过思想）、成为媒介中的社会参与者（不再是被动的观看者，获得交互性和控制感）以及媒介本身作为一种媒介。社会演员。

blocks.html_en: MUDs are also home to many players with vision impairments, who are attracted to the environment because they can potentially experience it fully. Visually-impaired players are often unable to play games that rely heavily on graphics, because they can struggle to interpret what is happening on the screen. However, in a text-based games, visually-impaired players can use a screenreader to read the contents of the terminal as events happen live on the screen. Many MUDs provide configuration options for visually-impaired players, allowing them to disable nonsensical text like ASCII art or character-based maps, and streamlining the text so that a screenreader can effectively convey the information to the player. A visually-impaired player with a high recitation speed on their screenreader can play just as effectively as a someone reading their screen, using sounds and triggers to facilitate their gameplay.

sentences.en: MUDs are also home to many players with vision impairments, who are attracted to the environment because they can potentially experience it fully.
sentences.zh: MUD 也是许多视力障碍玩家的家园，他们被环境所吸引，因为他们有可能充分体验它。

sentences.en: Visually-impaired players are often unable to play games that rely heavily on graphics, because they can struggle to interpret what is happening on the screen.
sentences.zh: 视障玩家通常无法玩严重依赖图形的游戏，因为他们很难理解屏幕上发生的事情。

sentences.en: However, in a text-based games, visually-impaired players can use a screenreader to read the contents of the terminal as events happen live on the screen.
sentences.zh: 然而，在基于文本的游戏中，视障玩家可以使用屏幕阅读器在屏幕上实时发生事件时阅读终端的内容。

sentences.en: Many MUDs provide configuration options for visually-impaired players, allowing them to disable nonsensical text like ASCII art or character-based maps, and streamlining the text so that a screenreader can effectively convey the information to the player.
sentences.zh: 许多 MUD 为视障玩家提供配置选项，允许他们禁用无意义的文本，例如 ASCII 艺术或基于字符的地图，并简化文本，以便屏幕阅读器可以有效地将信息传达给玩家。

sentences.en: A visually-impaired player with a high recitation speed on their screenreader can play just as effectively as a someone reading their screen, using sounds and triggers to facilitate their gameplay.
sentences.zh: 在屏幕阅读器上背诵速度较高的视障玩家可以像阅读屏幕的人一样有效地玩游戏，使用声音和触发器来促进他们的游戏。

blocks.html_en: In fact, until the invention of graphics cards and technologies that surpass the human imagination (haha!), MUDs will be difficult to completely remove from the scene. Imagine a realtime adventure with other people online, as vividly detailed as your favorite book but with all of the agency in your hands? That’s the reality of gameplay on a MUD. We put a lot of emphasis on graphics and visuals for games now, but as hardware begins to plateau, avenues like MUDs will continue to double-down on the experiences that make them so great, eschewing the bridles of resource creation in exchange for pure poiesis. You can spin up a MUD and start writing the environment, making it as intricate as you’d like, and everything you describe costs you the same amount of effort: words. Making a game with graphics is entirely different, with various effects, models, and landscapes requiring more time, attention, and skills than others. In a graphic-based game, not every asset has the same cost associated, and this can place artificial limitations on a game. Developers may opt for a less dramatic or exciting route simply because they lack the time or budget. Whereas in developing a MUD, your only limitation is your ability to describe.

sentences.en: In fact, until the invention of graphics cards and technologies that surpass the human imagination (haha!), MUDs will be difficult to completely remove from the scene.
sentences.zh: 事实上，在超越人类想象的显卡和技术发明之前（哈哈！），MUD 很难完全从场景中消失。

sentences.en: Imagine a realtime adventure with other people online, as vividly detailed as your favorite book but with all of the agency in your hands?
sentences.zh: 想象一下与其他人在线进行实时冒险，就像您最喜欢的书一样生动详细，但所有代理机构都在您手中？

sentences.en: That’s the reality of gameplay on a MUD.
sentences.zh: 这就是 MUD 游戏的现实。

sentences.en: We put a lot of emphasis on graphics and visuals for games now, but as hardware begins to plateau, avenues like MUDs will continue to double-down on the experiences that make them so great, eschewing the bridles of resource creation in exchange for pure poiesis.
sentences.zh: 我们现在非常重视游戏的图形和视觉效果，但随着硬件开始趋于稳定，像 MUD 这样的途径将继续加倍努力，以使其变得如此出色，避开资源创建的束缚，以换取纯粹的创作。

sentences.en: You can spin up a MUD and start writing the environment, making it as intricate as you’d like, and everything you describe costs you the same amount of effort: words.
sentences.zh: 您可以启动 MUD 并开始编写环境，使其变得像您想要的那样复杂，并且您描述的所有内容都会花费您相同的精力：文字。

sentences.en: Making a game with graphics is entirely different, with various effects, models, and landscapes requiring more time, attention, and skills than others.
sentences.zh: 制作带有图形的游戏是完全不同的，具有各种效果、模型和景观，需要比其他游戏更多的时间、注意力和技能。

sentences.en: In a graphic-based game, not every asset has the same cost associated, and this can place artificial limitations on a game.
sentences.zh: 在基于图形的游戏中，并非每个资产都具有相同的相关成本，这可能会对游戏造成人为的限制。

sentences.en: Developers may opt for a less dramatic or exciting route simply because they lack the time or budget.
sentences.zh: 开发人员可能会因为缺乏时间或预算而选择不那么引人注目或不那么令人兴奋的路线。

sentences.en: Whereas in developing a MUD, your only limitation is your ability to describe.
sentences.zh: 而在开发 MUD 时，您唯一的限制是您的描述能力。

blocks.html_en: Despite the age and legacy of this type of program, starting a MUD in 2018 can be a difficult process. There are many popular codebases that have been in use for years or even decades, and the command syntax, interfaces, and unique quirks of each of them means that players often develop preferences. These codebases can sometimes be in older programming languages or require complex accompanying databases to facilitate the program. Thankfully, there are codebases available that are fairly straightforward to set up and come with a fair amount of functionality out of the box. It’s worth noting that it can easily take months or years of development for even a team of dedicated creators to build a quality, developed MUD for players, and that’s barely considering the amount of writing and building that has to go into it. Creating unique and compelling gameplay mechanics that are balanced and engaging is a mixture of art and science that many MUDs still struggle to get right. Because I am a JavaScript junkie, I’ve done a fair amount of tinkering in a MUD engine called Ranvier and I recommend this codebase for modern would-be MUD wizards looking to set up a _tabula rasa _for tinkering. Because it takes such a large amount of time and dedication to create a MUD, I do not think there are any released/popular MUDs out there currently using the Ranvier engine, but the same can be said for many MUD codebases, unfortunately. Due to the nature of niche communities in which MUDs tend to live, it’s fairly common for these virtual worlds to appear, amass players, and then vanish just as quickly as they came. In some cases, the community can recover the source code and try to reboot, but other times the entire content is lost forever. Still, you can find a wide variety of popular MUDS ― some of which have been around for decades ― on websites like Top Mud Sites and The Mud Connector and I encourage you to check one out if any of this piques your interest.

sentences.en: Despite the age and legacy of this type of program, starting a MUD in 2018 can be a difficult process.
sentences.zh: 尽管此类项目已有很长的历史和遗产，但在 2018 年启动 MUD 可能是一个困难的过程。

sentences.en: There are many popular codebases that have been in use for years or even decades, and the command syntax, interfaces, and unique quirks of each of them means that players often develop preferences.
sentences.zh: 有许多流行的代码库已经使用了几年甚至几十年，它们的命令语法、界面和独特的怪癖意味着玩家经常会产生偏好。

sentences.en: These codebases can sometimes be in older programming languages or require complex accompanying databases to facilitate the program.
sentences.zh: 这些代码库有时可能采用较旧的编程语言，或者需要复杂的随附数据库来促进程序的进行。

sentences.en: Thankfully, there are codebases available that are fairly straightforward to set up and come with a fair amount of functionality out of the box.
sentences.zh: 值得庆幸的是，有一些可用的代码库设置起来相当简单，并且具有大量开箱即用的功能。

sentences.en: It’s worth noting that it can easily take months or years of development for even a team of dedicated creators to build a quality, developed MUD for players, and that’s barely considering the amount of writing and building that has to go into it.
sentences.zh: 值得注意的是，即使是由专门的创作者组成的团队也很容易需要数月或数年的开发时间才能为玩家构建高质量的、开发的 MUD，而这几乎没有考虑到必须进行的编写和构建的数量。

sentences.en: Creating unique and compelling gameplay mechanics that are balanced and engaging is a mixture of art and science that many MUDs still struggle to get right.
sentences.zh: 创造独特且引人入胜、平衡且引人入胜的游戏机制是艺术与科学的结合，许多 MUD 仍然难以做到这一点。

sentences.en: Because I am a JavaScript junkie, I’ve done a fair amount of tinkering in a MUD engine called Ranvier and I recommend this codebase for modern would-be MUD wizards looking to set up a _tabula rasa _for tinkering.
sentences.zh: 因为我是一个 JavaScript 迷，所以我在一个名为 Ranvier 的 MUD 引擎中做了相当多的修改，我向那些想要建立 _tabula rasa _ 进行修改的现代 MUD 向导推荐这个代码库。

sentences.en: Because it takes such a large amount of time and dedication to create a MUD, I do not think there are any released/popular MUDs out there currently using the Ranvier engine, but the same can be said for many MUD codebases, unfortunately.
sentences.zh: 因为创建 MUD 需要花费大量的时间和精力，所以我认为目前没有任何发布/流行的 MUD 使用 Ranvier 引擎，但不幸的是，对于许多 MUD 代码库来说也是如此。

sentences.en: Due to the nature of niche communities in which MUDs tend to live, it’s fairly common for these virtual worlds to appear, amass players, and then vanish just as quickly as they came.
sentences.zh: 由于 MUD 往往生活在利基社区的性质，这些虚拟世界出现、聚集玩家、然后消失的速度相当常见。

sentences.en: In some cases, the community can recover the source code and try to reboot, but other times the entire content is lost forever.
sentences.zh: 在某些情况下，社区可以恢复源代码并尝试重新启动，但其他时候整个内容将永远丢失。

sentences.en: Still, you can find a wide variety of popular MUDS ― some of which have been around for decades ― on websites like Top Mud Sites and The Mud Connector and I encourage you to check one out if any of this piques your interest.
sentences.zh: 尽管如此，您仍然可以在 Top Mud Sites 和 The Mud Connector 等网站上找到各种流行的 MUDS（其中一些已经存在了几十年），我鼓励您查看其中任何一个是否激起了您的兴趣。

blocks.html_en: You can read more about my new MUD engine project, Pinwheel, here on my blog.

sentences.en: You can read more about my new MUD engine project, Pinwheel, here on my blog.
sentences.zh: 您可以在我的博客上阅读有关我的新 MUD 引擎项目 Pinwheel 的更多信息。

sentences.zh: 进一步阅读

blocks.html_en: Ranvier, a MUD engine in JavaScript

sentences.en: Ranvier, a MUD engine in JavaScript
sentences.zh: Ranvier，JavaScript 中的 MUD 引擎

content_html_zh:
在智能设备和即时娱乐的时代，MUD这样古老的媒介能否生存？ 这些多用户维度（或地下城、域或许多其他选项，通常被称为 MU*）完全由文本驱动，是完全由文本组成的虚拟世界。 这些平台通常被设计成角色扮演游戏，同时也是网络边缘小众互联网社区的所在地。 这些服务器是通过 telnet 客户端访问的，该客户端很容易在命令行中启动。 但有一些流行的应用程序是许多长期玩家选择使用的。 它们很容易让人想起围绕 IRC 协议构建的各种聊天程序，并提供简单的功能，如日志记录、颜色、别名、触发器、计时器，甚至映射。

此时，如果你以前从未玩过MUD，你可能会对这个前景感到非常困惑。 完全是文字的在线游戏？ 它是如何运作的？ 更重要的是，为什么有人会玩这个？

当我十几岁的时候第一次偶然发现 MUD 时，这是我的第一反应。 我是一个狂热的读者，但玩一款完全由文字组成的游戏的想法感觉更像是工作而不是乐趣。 也许一开始确实感觉有点像工作，但我很快就掌握了控件，很快就感觉导航非常自然。 在典型的 MUD 设置中，您被表示为一个称为房间的空间内的文本化身。 房间有书面描述和房间内的物体列表。 房间可以是任何东西：狮子的巢穴、外太空、古老的地下室、教室、广阔的草原，甚至是珊瑚礁！ 您的化身可以通过您输入的命令与游戏世界及其内容进行交互。 这些可以像“拿剑”一样简单，但也可以像任何祈使句一样广泛。 一些 MUD 拥有强大的命令系统，允许您进行冗长的命令，例如“查看头饰上的宝石”或“驾驶船向北 500 米”，然后复杂的情况就会随之而来。

如果您曾经玩过互动小说 (IF)，那么您会对这些概念非常熟悉，互动小说可以最好地描述为 MUD 的单人游戏版本。 IF 和 MUD 之间有许多相似之处，但后者专注于实时环境中的协作（或竞争）工作。

在 MUD 中，环境不断变化，通过对刺激做出反应来产生场所感。 作为一个虚拟世界，它模拟具有不同复杂程度的各种系统，具体取决于代码库和环境设计。 这种不断搅动的模拟是 MUD 如此独特有趣的核心。 这些系统可以模拟时间、天气、经济、NPC 时间表等等——所有这些系统一起工作，创建一个互连的环境，可以由你的化身导航并通过动词进行交互。 这些模拟的复杂性在游戏和不同代码库之间可能存在很大差异。 一些 MUD 提供了更直接的砍杀环境，玩家可以收集物品并杀死怪物以获得经验并变得更强。 这些类型的游戏本质上是编程规则手册，取代了桌面游戏中 GM（游戏大师）的角色，而是处理所有战斗、掷骰子、技能培养和库存管理，否则您需要在桌子旁用笔和纸进行操作。 其他 MUD 提供完全自由的环境，没有规则、物品或 NPC。 这些环境中的玩家被提供了一个画布，并且通常期望在环境中进行角色扮演，与其他玩家协作编写故事——无论是否有游戏管理员的干预。 有些 MUD 介于两者之间，利用两全其美来提供独特的体验。

随着计算机与我们日常生活的联系越来越紧密，它们进一步成为我们操纵周围现实世界的工具。 例如，外科医生可以使用机械臂和相机从地球的另一侧进行手术。 通过技术与世界互动的独特感觉和现象可以赋予一种被定义为远程呈现的感觉存在。 远程呈现是指远程控制物体的体验，以及人们与虚拟环境互动并沉浸在虚拟环境中时的感觉。 在远程呈现的研究中，莱茵戈尔德等学者将MUD称为“计算机数据库中的想象世界，人们在其中使用文字和编程语言即兴创作情节剧，构建世界及其中的所有物体，解决谜题，发明娱乐和工具，争夺声望和权力，获得智慧，寻求复仇，放纵贪婪、欲望和暴力冲动。” Turkle 同样写道，“MUD 是一种新型的虚拟客厅游戏，也是一种新的社区形式。 此外，基于文本的 MUD 是一种新的协作撰写文学形式。 MUD玩家是MUD的作者、创作者以及媒体内容的消费者。 在这一点上，参与 MUD 与剧本创作、表演艺术、街头戏剧、即兴戏剧，甚至即兴喜剧有很多共同点。”

这种独特的远程呈现感觉可以有六种独特的形式，具体取决于用户的沉浸程度以及他们对所在虚拟环境的期望。 这些远程临场感包括社交丰富性（人们从社交互动中获得的感觉）、真实感（例如计算机生成的环境看起来、感觉或以其他方式看起来真实）、交通（还包括用户感觉好像有东西在他们身边或感觉好像他们正在与另一个人共享公共空间）、沉浸感（通过感官或通过思想）、成为媒介中的社会参与者（不再是被动的观看者，获得交互性和控制感）以及媒介本身作为一种媒介。社会演员。

MUD 也是许多视力障碍玩家的家园，他们被环境所吸引，因为他们有可能充分体验它。 视障玩家通常无法玩严重依赖图形的游戏，因为他们很难理解屏幕上发生的事情。 然而，在基于文本的游戏中，视障玩家可以使用屏幕阅读器在屏幕上实时发生事件时阅读终端的内容。 许多 MUD 为视障玩家提供配置选项，允许他们禁用无意义的文本，例如 ASCII 艺术或基于字符的地图，并简化文本，以便屏幕阅读器可以有效地将信息传达给玩家。 在屏幕阅读器上背诵速度较高的视障玩家可以像阅读屏幕的人一样有效地玩游戏，使用声音和触发器来促进他们的游戏。

事实上，在超越人类想象的显卡和技术发明之前（哈哈！），MUD 很难完全从场景中消失。 想象一下与其他人在线进行实时冒险，就像您最喜欢的书一样生动详细，但所有代理机构都在您手中？ 这就是 MUD 游戏的现实。 我们现在非常重视游戏的图形和视觉效果，但随着硬件开始趋于稳定，像 MUD 这样的途径将继续加倍努力，以使其变得如此出色，避开资源创建的束缚，以换取纯粹的创作。 您可以启动 MUD 并开始编写环境，使其变得像您想要的那样复杂，并且您描述的所有内容都会花费您相同的精力：文字。 制作带有图形的游戏是完全不同的，具有各种效果、模型和景观，需要比其他游戏更多的时间、注意力和技能。 在基于图形的游戏中，并非每个资产都具有相同的相关成本，这可能会对游戏造成人为的限制。 开发人员可能会因为缺乏时间或预算而选择不那么引人注目或不那么令人兴奋的路线。 而在开发 MUD 时，您唯一的限制是您的描述能力。

尽管此类项目已有很长的历史和遗产，但在 2018 年启动 MUD 可能是一个困难的过程。 有许多流行的代码库已经使用了几年甚至几十年，它们的命令语法、界面和独特的怪癖意味着玩家经常会产生偏好。 这些代码库有时可能采用较旧的编程语言，或者需要复杂的随附数据库来促进程序的进行。 值得庆幸的是，有一些可用的代码库设置起来相当简单，并且具有大量开箱即用的功能。 值得注意的是，即使是由专门的创作者组成的团队也很容易需要数月或数年的开发时间才能为玩家构建高质量的、开发的 MUD，而这几乎没有考虑到必须进行的编写和构建的数量。 创造独特且引人入胜、平衡且引人入胜的游戏机制是艺术与科学的结合，许多 MUD 仍然难以做到这一点。 因为我是一个 JavaScript 迷，所以我在一个名为 Ranvier 的 MUD 引擎中做了相当多的修改，我向那些想要建立 _tabula rasa _ 进行修改的现代 MUD 向导推荐这个代码库。 因为创建 MUD 需要花费大量的时间和精力，所以我认为目前没有任何发布/流行的 MUD 使用 Ranvier 引擎，但不幸的是，对于许多 MUD 代码库来说也是如此。 由于 MUD 往往生活在利基社区的性质，这些虚拟世界出现、聚集玩家、然后消失的速度相当常见。 在某些情况下，社区可以恢复源代码并尝试重新启动，但其他时候整个内容将永远丢失。 尽管如此，您仍然可以在 Top Mud Sites 和 The Mud Connector 等网站上找到各种流行的 MUDS（其中一些已经存在了几十年），我鼓励您查看其中任何一个是否激起了您的兴趣。

您可以在我的博客上阅读有关我的新 MUD 引擎项目 Pinwheel 的更多信息。
